name: Chocolate Mint Pinwheels
recipeIngredient: 1 package NESTLE Toll House mint flavored semi-sweet chocolate morsels, divided
recipeInstructions:
1. Over hot (not boiling) water, melt half of the Nestle Toll House mint flavored semi-sweet chocolate morsels, stirring until smooth. Cool to room temperature; set aside.
2. In a large mixing bowl, beat together butter, sugar, and salt until creamy. Beat in egg and vanilla extract (mixture may look curdled). Gradually add flour.
3. Place one cup of dough into a medium bowl; blend in the reserved melted morsels. Shape each dough into a ball; flatten and cover with plastic wrap. Refrigerate until firm, about 1 1/2 hours.
4. Preheat oven to 375°F. Between sheets of waxed paper, roll each ball of dough into a 13x9-inch rectangle. Remove the top layers of waxed paper. Invert the chocolate dough onto the plain dough. Peel off the remaining waxed paper.
5. Starting with a long side, roll up the rectangle in jelly-roll style. Cut into 1/4-inch slices; place on ungreased cookie sheets.
6. Bake for 7-10 minutes. Let stand on cookie sheets for 2 minutes. Remove from cookie sheets and cool completely.
7. Over hot (not boiling) water, melt the remaining Nestle Toll House mint flavored semi-sweet chocolate morsels, stirring until smooth. Spread a flat side of each cookie with slightly rounded 1/2 teaspoonful melted chocolate.
8. Refrigerate for 10 minutes to set chocolate. Makes 3 1/2 dozen cookies.
